boundaries: candidate repository reads limited to public metadata, README/license excerpts, and repository metadata
sources_reviewed: QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench LICENSE fetch result: 404 Not Found

reviews.name: modelcontextprotocol/servers
reviews.license_evidence: LICENSE states the project is transitioning from MIT to Apache-2.0, with documentation contributions excluding specifications under CC-BY-4.0.
runtime_security_surface: filesystem, git, fetch, memory, and tool/data access reference patterns
explicit non-production/reference warning and threat-model requirement
reviews.redistribution_boundary: Metadata/source-profile review only. Do not copy server code or executable snippets into package-facing skills. Any future normalization should extract boundary patterns and safety heuristics, not implementation content.

reviews.name: vercel/ai
reviews.license_evidence: LICENSE is Apache-2.0. packages/ai/README.md describes the package as a provider-agnostic TypeScript toolkit for AI-powered applications and agents.
reviews.redistribution_boundary: Source profile only in this pass. Do not copy examples, prompts, or implementation code. Future normalization may write original guidance about provider abstraction and sandbox/tool safety with attribution.

reviews.name: QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench
reviews.license_evidence: LICENSE fetch returned 404 on the default branch. README states a repo-level benchmark/tooling suite and lists benchmark data, task definitions, repositories, ground truth, test scripts, and reports.
runtime_security_surface: bundled task repositories and ground-truth/evaluation assets
reviews.redistribution_boundary: Do not normalize, package, copy, or derive dataset/task content until a license/redistribution boundary is directly verified.

reviews.name: openclaw/openclaw
reviews.license_evidence: LICENSE is MIT and records third-party notices separately. README describes real messaging channels, local-first gateway, multi-agent routing, tools, cron, browser/canvas/nodes, pairing defaults, and sandbox defaults.
runtime_security_surface: gateway exposure and remote access surfaces
host tool access in main session
reviews.redistribution_boundary: Source profile only. No normalization or package-facing endorsement until a dedicated high-risk source review decides which safety patterns can be abstracted without operational account/browser/cron mutation detail.

acceptance_tests.test: verifies license/redistribution boundary for each review_next candidate
acceptance_tests.evidence: modelcontextprotocol/servers, vercel/ai, and openclaw/openclaw license files were directly fetched; GitTaskBench LICENSE fetch returned 404 and was left watch/license-blocked.

acceptance_tests.test: classifies safety/runtime surface, including OpenClaw high-risk messaging/browser/cron/account surfaces
acceptance_tests.evidence: OpenClaw was classified high risk due to real messaging/account/channel integrations, browser/canvas/nodes/cron tools, gateway exposure, host tool access, and sandbox policy surfaces.

acceptance_tests.test: creates source profiles, skip/watch notes, risk items, or normalization queues only when justified
acceptance_tests.evidence: Created source profiles for three license-resolved candidates, one watch note for license-blocked GitTaskBench, one high-risk OpenClaw review queue, and one bounded Vercel AI SDK normalization queue.

acceptance_tests.test: does not copy third-party content, clone repositories, execute code, publish, or package candidates
acceptance_tests.evidence: No repositories were cloned; no candidate code was executed; no third-party implementation content was copied; no skill, catalog, package, npm, or publication action occurred.

value_delta: Converted metadata-only discovery candidates into reviewed source profiles, a license-blocked watch note, a high-risk OpenClaw review gate, and one bounded low-risk normalization path without copying or executing third-party content.
